candidate_problems.text: How can the boundary B_A be defined non-arbitrarily and shown to be an invariant of the architecture rather than an artifact of the chosen coding scheme Σ, proof system, or resource model? The conjecture depends on a robust decomposition into decidable, approximate, and undecidable self-facts, but it is unresolved whether that trichotomy survives changes in representation or collapses under alternative encodings.

Which predicate families Φ admit simultaneously attainable, sound, and complete self-knowledge under explicit resource bounds, and is there a principled characterization of the maximal such class? This is the central classification problem left open by the conjecture, connecting expressivity, proof-theoretic strength, and computational complexity.

What exact notion of 'internal certification' is load-bearing for the incompleteness result, especially for probabilistic, learned, or approximate self-models? It remains unclear whether the obstruction class extends cleanly beyond classical theorem-proving systems to agents whose self-ascriptions are statistical, corrigible, or behaviorally grounded rather than deductively certified.
